quiz.title: High-School Geometry: Unit 3 Quiz 6
quiz.subtitle: Calculating Steepness and Rate of Change
quiz.subject: Geometry

questions.question: What is the slope of the line passing through $(2, 3)$ and $(6, 11)$?
questions.explanation: The slope formula is $m = \frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1}$. Substituting: $m = \frac{11 - 3}{6 - 2} = \frac{8}{4} = 2$.

questions.question: What is the slope of the line passing through $(-1, 5)$ and $(3, -3)$?
A line has a slope of $0$. What does this indicate about the line?
options: The line rises from left to right
The line falls from left to right
questions.explanation: A slope of $0$ means there is no vertical change between any two points on the line — it is perfectly horizontal. All points on the line share the same $y$-coordinate.

questions.question: A line has an undefined slope. What does this indicate about the line?
options: The line rises from left to right
The line falls from left to right
questions.explanation: A vertical line has an undefined slope because the denominator $x_2 - x_1$ equals $0$, and division by zero is undefined. All points on a vertical line share the same $x$-coordinate.

questions.question: What is the slope of the line passing through $(4, -2)$ and $(4, 7)$?
questions.explanation: Both points have the same $x$-coordinate, so the line is vertical. The slope is undefined because $x_2 - x_1 = 4 - 4 = 0$, and division by zero is undefined.

questions.question: A line rises from left to right. What must be true about its slope?
questions.explanation: A line that rises as it moves from left to right has a positive slope. As $x$ increases, $y$ also increases.

questions.question: What is the slope of the line passing through $(-3, 4)$ and $(5, 4)$?
questions.explanation: Both points have the same $y$-coordinate, so the line is horizontal. The slope is $0$ because $y_2 - y_1 = 4 - 4 = 0$.

questions.question: What is the slope of the line passing through $(0, 0)$ and $(-5, 10)$?
Two lines are parallel. Line $\ell$ has a slope of $\frac{3}{4}$. What is the slope of line $m$?
questions.explanation: Parallel lines have equal slopes. Since line $\ell$ has a slope of $\frac{3}{4}$, line $m$ must also have a slope of $\frac{3}{4}$.

questions.question: The slope of a line is $-\frac{2}{5}$. Which statement about the line is true?
options: The line falls $2$ units for every $5$ units it moves to the right
The line rises $2$ units for every $5$ units it moves to the right
The line falls $5$ units for every $2$ units it moves to the right
questions.explanation: A slope of $-\frac{2}{5}$ means that for every $5$ units of horizontal movement to the right, the line falls $2$ units vertically. The negative sign indicates a downward direction.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered calculating slope and interpreting what the slope reveals about a line's direction and steepness.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand the slope formula but review the cases of zero slope (horizontal line) and undefined slope (vertical line).
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Focus on: slope $= \frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1}$, positive slope rises, negative slope falls, zero slope is horizontal, and undefined slope is vertical.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! Slope measures the rate of vertical change per unit of horizontal change. Parallel lines have equal slopes. A horizontal line has slope $0$; a vertical line has undefined slope.
